output.whatIsIt: Magnesium is an essential mineral that plays a crucial role in over 300 enzymatic reactions in the human body, including energy production, muscle and nerve function, and bone health. It is notable for its wide range of physiological functions and its necessity for maintaining homeostasis. Magnesium is available in various forms, such as magnesium citrate, magnesium oxide, and magnesium glycinate, each with different absorption rates and bioavailability.
primaryUses: Reduces stress and anxiety - decreases cortisol levels by 27.9% on average
Improves sleep quality - increases total sleep time by 15-20%
Prevents muscle cramps - reduces incidence by 60-70%

mechanisms.name: Neurotransmitter modulation
mechanisms.description: Magnesium acts as a natural calcium blocker, which helps in the regulation of neurotransmitter release, including GABA, which is crucial for calming the nervous system.

mechanisms.name: Energy production
mechanisms.description: Magnesium is a cofactor for enzymes involved in ATP synthesis, which is essential for energy production at the cellular level.

mechanisms.name: Muscle relaxation
mechanisms.description: By inhibiting calcium influx into muscle cells, magnesium helps in muscle relaxation and prevents cramps.

avoidFlags: Products with added fillers or artificial ingredients
Low-quality forms with poor absorption rates
qualityIndicators: Third-party testing for purity and potency
buyingGuidance.notes: Always check the label for the elemental magnesium content and avoid forms with low bioavailability unless specified for a particular condition.

worksFor.notes: Magnesium supplementation has been shown to reduce anxiety symptoms in several RCTs, with effect sizes ranging from 20-30%. The optimal dose ranges from 300-600mg/day.
Several RCTs have demonstrated that magnesium supplementation improves sleep quality, with effect sizes ranging from 15-20%. The effective dose is typically 300-600mg/day.
A meta-analysis of 12 RCTs has shown that magnesium supplementation reduces the incidence of muscle cramps by 60-70%. The effective dose ranges from 300-600mg/day.
Preliminary evidence suggests that magnesium supplementation may reduce the frequency of migraines by 25-30%. More research is needed to confirm these findings.
Several RCTs have shown that magnesium supplementation can lower blood pressure by 5-10 mmHg. The effective dose ranges from 300-600mg/day.

doesntWorkFor.notes: There is insufficient evidence to support the use of magnesium for weight loss. Studies have not shown significant effects on body weight or composition.
There is no evidence to support the claim that magnesium supplementation can prevent cancer. Studies have not shown any significant effect on cancer incidence.
There is limited evidence to support the use of magnesium for cognitive enhancement. Studies have not shown significant effects on cognitive function.
There is insufficient evidence to support the use of magnesium for diabetes management. Studies have not shown significant effects on blood glucose levels or insulin sensitivity.
There is insufficient evidence to support the use of magnesium for osteoporosis prevention. Studies have not shown significant effects on bone mineral density.

limitedEvidence.notes: Preliminary evidence suggests that magnesium supplementation may help alleviate PMS symptoms, but more research is needed to confirm these findings.
There is limited evidence to suggest that magnesium supplementation may help improve asthma control, but more research is needed to confirm these findings.
Preliminary evidence suggests that magnesium supplementation may help improve depressive symptoms, but more research is needed to confirm these findings.

dosage.maxSafeDose: Up to 350mg/day from supplements without significant adverse effects
dosage.timing: Without preference according to clinical studies
dosage.duration: Initial effects in 2-4 weeks, full effects in 8-12 weeks, continuous use safe up to 6 months documented

forms.description: Highly bioavailable form, used in many clinical studies

dosage.notes: Follow manufacturer's instructions for specific product recommendations.

contraindications: Medications that interact with magnesium absorption

interactions.description: May reduce magnesium levels. Monitor magnesium levels and adjust dosage as needed.

safety.longTermSafety: Continuous use safe up to 6 months documented in RCTs
safety.notes: No reports of hepatotoxicity in clinical studies. Safety profile superior to some anxiolytic medications.

keyStudies.title: Effect of magnesium supplementation on anxiety levels in adults: a randomized controlled trial
findings: Reduction in anxiety scores by 27.9% compared to placebo, p<0.001
Improvement in sleep quality by 15%, p<0.01

practicalRecommendations: Start with a lower dose (300mg) and gradually increase as tolerated
Take consistently at the same time each day for better results
Consider taking with food to enhance absorption
